elements.title: Europejara
elements.contents: ... that Europejara (head pictured) is the oldest known toothless pterosaur?

elements.title: Joyce Castle
elements.contents: ... that opera singer Joyce Castle specialized in "portraying nuts, mamas, lesbians, nymphomaniacs, witches, and men"?

elements.title: Khirbet el-Maqatir
elements.contents: ... that residents of Khirbet el-Maqatir during the Jewish–Roman wars secretly cut a passage between their hiding cave and a water cistern so they could draw water unseen while hiding from the Romans?

elements.title: Benjamin Naylor
elements.contents: ... that merchant Benjamin Naylor built a store that contributed to the small New Zealand town of Clyde being described as having "movie-set good looks"?

elements.title: an old factory
elements.contents: ... that fur-trading ships wintered in an old factory?

elements.title: Eduardo Clark
elements.contents: ... that Eduardo Clark discovered that several teachers registered on Mexico's national education payroll were collecting more than MXN$500,000 per month, with some listed at least ten times?

elements.title: a derailment in 2005
elements.contents: ... that a derailment in 2005 was caused by a train attempting to travel a 10 mph (16 km/h) crossing at 69 mph (111 km/h)?

elements.title: Volition
elements.contents: ... that the video game developer Volition was named after a lexical definition?

elements.title: My Little Pony porn
elements.contents: ... that My Little Pony porn has been studied by scholars?
